meaning: vi.粘紧,附着,紧贴,坚持(意见),墨守(习惯)
examples: Since you don't like your perception of him, why cling to it?
He had one last hope to cling on to.
The cat tried to cling to the edge by its claws.
Economists tend to cling to whatever assumptions are required to use the techniques they favour.
Some of the smaller ones throw their arms around your waist and cling to you.
